metadata.note: 75 decide statements ingested into DB; 25 test queries are paraphrases of 25 train entries with ground truth labels.

train.content: The club approved $1,200 for the spring kickoff event, covering $800 for catering and $400 for venue decorations.
We voted to cap annual membership dues at $40 per student, effective next semester.
The club has decided to hold general meetings every other Tuesday at 6pm in Building C Room 204.
Approved: purchase of a $350 portable projector and $60 HDMI adapters for presentations.
The club will co-host the end-of-year gala with the Marketing Society. Costs split 50/50.
We decided to require officers to give two weeks notice before stepping down from their role.
The club voted to use Notion as the official documentation platform, replacing Google Docs.
Approved a $250 budget for printing and distributing flyers for the recruitment drive.
We have decided to introduce a mentorship program pairing first-year members with senior executives.
The club approved sending two representatives to the regional conference in March at a cost of $600.
Voted to require a quorum of at least 50% of active members for any budget vote to be valid.
The club agreed to host a networking night on the last Friday of each semester.
We decided that all reimbursement requests must be submitted within 30 days of the expense.
Approved $500 for custom club merchandise: 50 hoodies at $10 each.
The club voted to create a social media committee of three members responsible for Instagram and LinkedIn posts.
We agreed that all events must be planned and documented at least two weeks in advance.
The club approved $150 for monthly coffee chats with industry professionals.
Decided to freeze new officer elections until the constitution committee finishes the bylaw revision.
The club will partner with the Career Centre to offer resume review workshops twice a semester.
Approved the purchase of $80 worth of whiteboard markers and flip-chart paper for workshops.
We decided to introduce a three-strike attendance policy: members missing three consecutive meetings lose voting rights.
The club approved $900 for the winter formal event: $600 venue rental and $300 for catering.
Voted to move all club announcements from email to the Slack workspace going forward.
The club agreed to create an onboarding package for new members including a welcome letter and FAQ.
Approved $200 for a subscription to a design tool to produce professional club posters.
We decided that the VP Finance must present a monthly budget report at each general meeting.
The club voted to hold an annual leadership retreat for officers at the start of the fall semester.
Approved $75 for name tags and printed agendas for the annual general meeting.
We agreed to implement a peer-review process for all written content published on the club website.
The club decided to offer two types of membership: general membership (free) and premium membership ($25) with extra perks.
Voted to donate 5% of any fundraising surplus to a local student emergency fund.
The club approved $400 for a welcome-back BBQ at the start of the fall semester.
We decided to record all general meetings and post the recordings to the club drive within 48 hours.
Approved a $300 grant from the student union to cover printing costs for the club journal.
The club agreed to run a case-competition team this semester with a $200 prep budget.
Voted to require all officers to complete the university's financial accountability training by September.
We decided to invite a panel of alumni to speak at the spring networking event.
The club approved $120 for a gift card raffle prize at the end-of-year celebration.
Decided to create a shared Google Calendar visible to all club members for events and deadlines.
The club voted to cap workshop attendance at 30 students to maintain quality.
Approved $550 for professional photography at the annual gala.
We agreed to host a panel discussion on career paths in consulting every fall semester.
The club decided that all sponsorship proposals must be reviewed by the VP Finance before submission.
Voted to allocate $180 for team-building escape room activity for exec members.
We decided to launch a weekly newsletter summarising industry news for club members.
The club approved a $700 catering budget for the mid-semester industry mixer.
Voted to establish a complaints process where members can anonymously flag concerns to the president.
We agreed that the club logo and brand colours may not be altered without a majority vote.
The club decided to set up a $500 emergency fund in the club account for unexpected expenses.
Approved $160 for books and reading materials for the club's professional development library.
The club voted to require all members to sign a code of conduct at the start of each year.
We decided to hold a hackathon in collaboration with the Computer Science Society next semester.
The club approved $220 for prizes at the hackathon: $100 first place, $80 second, $40 third.
Voted to post all meeting minutes on the club website within 72 hours of each meeting.
We agreed to reduce general meeting frequency to once a month during exam periods.
The club decided to apply for the student government's Club Excellence Award this spring.
Approved $90 for a cake and balloons for the end-of-year officer appreciation celebration.
We decided that any unspent funds at year-end must be carried over, not redistributed.
The club voted to host a skills-sharing session where members teach a topic of their choice monthly.
Approved: the club will open officer elections to all members with at least one full semester of membership.
We decided to stop selling physical tickets and switch to a free Eventbrite sign-up for all events.
The club approved $350 for a team dinner to celebrate hitting 100 members.
Voted to mandate that exec meetings run on a strict 60-minute agenda to respect everyone's time.
We agreed to set up a Linktree page linking to all club resources, updated each semester.
The club approved $480 for two tickets and registration fees for the national case competition.
Decided to form a diversity and inclusion committee to organise at least one cultural event per semester.
The club voted to require sponsorship agreements to be signed at least four weeks before the sponsored event.
We approved $130 for a Zoom Pro subscription to support hybrid meetings.
The club agreed that all social media posts must be approved by the VP Communications before going live.
Approved a $600 speaker honorarium for a CFO to present at the finance careers night.
We decided to open the club's general meetings to non-members as guests, limited to two visits before joining.
The club voted to use an anonymous online poll to gather feedback after every major event.
Approved $275 for branded tote bags to give to attendees at the spring career fair booth.
We agreed to maintain a waitlist for oversubscribed workshops and notify waitlisted members 24 hours before.
The club decided to hold a town hall at the start of each semester to set goals and gather member input.

test.query: What budget was set for the spring kickoff event?
test.note: paraphrase of train:001 — spring kickoff catering/decor budget

test.query: How much do members have to pay to join?
test.note: paraphrase of train:002 — membership dues cap

test.query: When and where do general meetings take place?
test.note: paraphrase of train:003 — meeting schedule and location

test.query: Did we buy any presentation equipment?
test.note: paraphrase of train:004 — projector and HDMI purchase

test.query: What do officers have to do before resigning?
test.note: paraphrase of train:006 — officer notice period policy

test.query: What platform does the club use for documentation?
test.note: paraphrase of train:007 — Notion as docs platform

test.query: How does the mentorship program work?
test.note: paraphrase of train:009 — mentorship program pairing

test.query: How many people need to show up for a budget vote to count?
test.note: paraphrase of train:011 — quorum requirement for votes

test.query: What is the deadline to submit expense reimbursements?
test.note: paraphrase of train:013 — 30-day reimbursement policy

test.query: How much was spent on club hoodies?
test.note: paraphrase of train:014 — merchandise/hoodie budget

test.query: What happens if a member misses too many meetings?
test.note: paraphrase of train:021 — attendance strike policy

test.query: Where should members look for club announcements?
test.note: paraphrase of train:023 — announcements moved to Slack

test.query: What financial reports does the VP Finance need to give?
test.note: paraphrase of train:026 — monthly budget report requirement

test.query: What is the difference between general and premium membership?
test.note: paraphrase of train:030 — two-tier membership tiers

test.query: Are meeting recordings available to members?
test.note: paraphrase of train:033 — meeting recording policy

test.query: What is the club doing to help members prepare for case competitions?
test.note: paraphrase of train:035 — case competition team and budget

test.query: Is there a limit on how many people can attend a workshop?
test.note: paraphrase of train:040 — workshop attendance cap

test.query: Who needs to approve a sponsorship deal before it's submitted?
test.note: paraphrase of train:043 — sponsorship proposal review policy

test.query: Can I raise a concern about something anonymously?
test.note: paraphrase of train:047 — anonymous complaints process

test.query: How long can exec meetings run?
test.note: paraphrase of train:063 — 60-minute exec meeting cap

test.query: What was approved for the national case competition trip?
test.note: paraphrase of train:065 — competition tickets and registration

test.query: Who approves social media content before it goes out?
test.note: paraphrase of train:069 — VP Communications approval policy

test.query: How much was the guest speaker paid for the finance careers night?
test.note: paraphrase of train:070 — CFO honorarium

test.query: Can non-members come to general meetings?
test.note: paraphrase of train:071 — guest policy for non-members

test.query: What happens to leftover money at the end of the year?
test.note: paraphrase of train:058 — unspent funds carryover policy
